rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical diazolation of multisubstituted alkenes with pyrazoles to form N,N'-ethylene-bridged bispyrazoles, with H2 as the sole byproduct
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the alkene to an alkene radical cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: alkenyl radical cation A (from one-electron oxidation of alkene 1)
β-pyrazolyl carbon radical B (after nucleophilic addition of pyrazole to A and deprotonation)
carbocation intermediate C (from second anodic oxidation of B)
N,N'-ethylene-bridged bispyrazole product 3 (after second pyrazole addition and deprotonation)

electron_flow: The anode first oxidizes the alkene to a radical cation A, which is trapped by pyrazole and deprotonated to give C-radical B; a second anodic oxidation converts B to cation C, which is captured by a second pyrazole and deprotonated to deliver the bispyrazole, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Initial anodic oxidation of the alkene (suggested by CV: oxidation peak of alkene 1a shifts from ~1.5 V to ~1.1–1.3 V vs SCE in presence of pyrazole)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (1r)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction, indicating a second substituent / specific substitution pattern on the alkene is required

probe_reagents.name: stilbene (1q)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product, supporting substrate-scope/mechanistic constraints

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and 1a+2a
probe_reagents.observation: 2a alone inert; 1a oxidizes at Ep/2 ≈ +1.5 V vs SCE; mixture shifts onset to ~1.1 V and Ep/2 to +1.3 V, indicating pyrazole promotes alkene oxidation

notes: Mechanism is proposed based mainly on CV and literature precedent; no radical-trap (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT studies were reported. Gram-scale experiment (0.865 g, 55%) demonstrates scalability. Alternative pathways (e.g., direct two-electron oxidation to a vinyl cation, or initial pyrazole oxidation) are not explicitly ruled out, though CV shows pyrazole 2a is inert in the operating window.
